channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan mediates high-level US-Iran peace talks amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Pakistan has stepped into the international spotlight as a mediator between the US and Iran, hosting high-level peace talks in Islamabad aimed at halting ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Following...

item.title: Investigation launched by Manhattan DA into sexual assault allegations against U.S. Representative Eric Swalwell
item.description: The Manhattan District Attorney's office has confirmed that it is investigating allegations of sexual assault against U.S. Representative Eric Swalwell, a Democrat from California who is also a leadin...

item.title: Father in Eastern France Charged After Nine-Year-Old Found Locked and Malnourished in Van
item.description: A French man has been charged after police in the eastern village of Hagenbach discovered his nine-year-old son living in appalling conditions inside a locked van. Authorities were alerted when a neig...

item.title: BTS shares laughter and honesty in their guesting on ‘Hot Ones’
item.description: In their latest appearance on the popular YouTube talk show ‘Hot Ones,’ the global K-pop sensation BTS showed a more relaxed and playful side, giving fans a peek into their personalities beyond the sp...

item.title: Man Arrested in Connection with James Bulger Grave Vandalism
item.description: Police have arrested a 78-year-old man following an incident of vandalism at the grave of James Bulger in Merseyside. The arrest was made after reports of damage to the grave site, a place that has be...

item.title: Tinubu urges Nigerians to show gratitude despite rising fuel prices
item.description: President Bola Tinubu, during a visit to Bayelsa State on April 10, 2026, urged Nigerians to express gratitude despite the severe fuel price hike that has worsened economic conditions in the country. ...

item.title: Making Short Trips Safer and Easier by Bicycle in Canberra
item.description: Many Canberrans are finding that riding bicycles for short trips is not only practical but often quicker than using public transport. According to the Household Travel Survey, cycling a kilometre take...

item.title: Nigeria Police Force Gives Financial Support to Families of Deceased Officers in Rivers State
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force (NPF) don release a total of ?21,678,120 to the families of police officers wey don die while on duty for Rivers State. This na part of ongoing welfare programmes wey the forc...

item.title: South Africa plans tougher immigration rules to curb asylum law abuse
item.description: South Africa’s Department of Home Affairs is proposing major changes to its immigration, citizenship, and refugee protection laws. The department aims to address what it calls 'abuse' of the current a...

item.title: Liberals support non-binding proposal to restrict social media access for youth under 16
item.description: The Liberal Party of Canada has voted in favour of a non-binding motion that calls for banning Canadians under the age of 16 from using social media platforms. The resolution, passed by party members ...

item.title: Government Launches Nairobi Metropolitan Police Unit to Enhance City Security
item.description: The Kenyan government is moving fast to establish a Nairobi Metropolitan Police Unit after President William Ruto issued a directive to make the capital city safer. Interior CS Kipchumba Murkomen conf...

item.title: Ebonyi Catholic Diocese Mourns Passing of Bishop Peter Chukwu
item.description: Di people for Ebonyi State don dey mourn the passing of Most Rev. Peter Chukwu, Bishop of Abakaliki Catholic Diocese, wey die on Friday, April 10, 2026, at di age of 60. Di Vicar General, Rev. Fr. Don...

item.title: Yulo Falls Short of Podium in Croatia Floor Exercise
item.description: Filipino gymnast Eldrew Yulo unfortunately missed securing a spot on the podium in the floor exercise event during a FIG World Cup competition held in Croatia. While Yulo consistently demonstrates rem...

item.title: Quebec phases out grocery store bottle deposit machines in favour of specialized sorting centres
item.description: Quebec is modernizing its beverage container deposit-refund system by phasing out reverse vending machines located inside grocery stores. These machines, operated by Consignaction, are being replaced ...

item.title: Nigerian Government Imposes Sanctions on Individuals Linked to Terrorism Financing
item.description: Di Nigerian government under President Bola Tinubu don release new sanctions list wey cover Tukur Mamu, Simon Ekpa, and 46 other people and organizations due to dem alleged connection to terrorism fin...

item.title: Sam Altman addresses New Yorker profile and recent attack at his home
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman published a blog post on Friday evening after a troubling incident at his San Francisco home and in response to a critical New Yorker profile. According to police reports, someon...

item.title: ACT prison population rises steadily, government dashboard provides detailed data
item.description: The average monthly population at the Alexander Maconochie Centre, the ACT's only adult prison, has surged to 493 detainees in March 2026, marking a 25 per cent increase since March 2023. The rise has...

item.title: Senators Shut Out Islanders 3-0, Nearing Playoff Qualification
item.description: The Ottawa Senators delivered a commanding performance against the New York Islanders, winning 3-0 and positioning themselves on the cusp of clinching a playoff spot. Ridly Greig opened the scoring wi...

item.title: Canadian Liberal Party Navigates Internal Challenges as MP Switches Allegiances
item.description: The Canadian Liberal Party is currently grappling with internal challenges, marked by the recent defection of a Member of Parliament (MP) to a newly formed political party. This departure follows a pe...

item.title: Linux Laptop Browser Comparison: Firefox and Chrome Performance in 2026
item.description: A Phoronix benchmark comparison, conducted in April 2026, evaluated the performance of Firefox and Google Chrome on a Linux laptop running Ubuntu 26.04. The test utilized JetStream 3.0, a web browser ...
